message:
Fix double-free in parser by immediately allocating on heap

The previous errdefer pattern caused double-frees when:
1. An expression was parsed (e.g., condition_expr)
2. It was copied to a heap-allocated pointer (condition_ptr)
3. An error occurred - both errdefers tried to free the same data

Fix: Immediately allocate on heap after parsing, with explicit error
handling for the allocation failure case. This ensures only one owner
exists at any time.

Changes:
- parseCall: Allocate func_ptr first, handle allocation error explicitly
- parseAttribute: Use catch block instead of errdefer for value cleanup
- parseSubscript: Same pattern - immediate allocation with catch
- parseIf: Wrap condition/elif parsing in blocks, allocate immediately
- Remove problematic errdefers that caused double ownership

Flask import now has 9 leaks (from unsupported syntax errors) instead
of double-free crashes. Simple files and blinker still have 0 leaks.
